items.prompt: interprets improper fractions as quantities that may exceed one whole
items.expected_evidence: explain why 5/4 represents one whole and one fourth more
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner completes the direct task accurately.

items.prompt: connects mixed numbers and improper fractions in bounded equivalent cases
items.expected_evidence: match bounded mixed numbers and improper fractions that describe the same quantity
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the concept coherently.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to one_whole_boundary_confusion.
items.expected_evidence: compare simple magnitudes greater than one whole without reverting to whole-number-only logic
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the source of the error and states the correct reasoning.

items.prompt: succeeds across visual models, symbolic forms, and early number-line situations
items.expected_evidence: use whole-and-part composition language for fraction quantities beyond one
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner succeeds in a less-familiar context.
